All right, welcome back. Things are about to get really exciting and also really real. Really real. Anyway, here's the deal. So, in this lesson now, what you're going to see is what happens when someone with low standards starts performing above their expectations and in fact exceeds their own standard. What happens? How they self-sabotage? How it undermines their own success and they don't even know it. So it's almost the same way that beliefs are these silent killers of success sitting just below the surface while standards operate in a very similar fashion but they're almost even more insidious because it's not any one belief. It's just the sum of all the beliefs and values and experiences that define this standard and then if you don't hit that standard you feel angst and if you exceed that standard you're like, whoa, I better slow down. So what Sarah uncovers here is how that problem plays out in the real world and that sets you up now for the solution which we'll talk about after but I'll see you on the other side. Let's watch this now. It's about five or six minutes. So how do standards actually apply to the straight line? The question, so they apply to the straight line in the sense that if you don't have a high standard the straight line is so powerful like guess what? You know what you'll do? I guess this might not be bad for some people. You won't have to work that much to get rich because you can make a lot of money really quickly because when you can close with the straight line system you're very valuable commodity in the workplace. So yeah, if you want to and here's the thing, I'm going to take that back for a second and say there's nothing wrong with that. If that's your vision for the future, see the question really is what's your vision? Where do you see yourself in five years from now and why? If your five year vision and the goals that connect up to that, if those together as a whole are congruent with a standard that says I need to make 100,000 a year then God bless you. Seriously, nothing wrong with that at all. But I caution you that we live in an expensive world and $100,000 a year, it's very tough being this, you know, we're going to call taxes. It's $50,000 really, maybe even less and then what happens if you get sick and a medical condition? Seriously, this is what happens. I don't believe $100,000 is a rational number. But hey, Mother Teresa was a lot richer than I ever was because she was her standard was what? How many people she could help? Her vision for the future was I want to make the world pain free and just give love and light, right? And she succeeded while she was broke her whole life. She was richer than I ever was because for her, she had perfect congruency between what her standard was and what her vision was. So there's no right or wrong answer, but chances are I'm guessing if you're taking this course right now, you're probably not Mother Teresa's awesome as she was. You're probably a bit more on the money centric side. Maybe you work at a company, right? And you're trying to enhance your skills, has to move up the corporate level, ever it might be all that matters is that a, use a straight line ethically, be that you get congruency in your life between your vision for the future and your own personal standard and see that you use the power from this to lift everybody else up around you. If you do that, you are living a first class life and you truly are rich. All right, powerful stuff, right? So here we go. We got to get active right now in the exercise area here. After you go through your quiz, these questions are designed to specifically ensure that you get honest right now about exactly where you are in terms of your standard. Is your standard serving you or is it not serving you? This is what we're going to find that out. Now, also, obviously to do this, we need to go back to an earlier module. To what? To vision, right? We need to now take out that vision that you created. Where do you see yourself in five years from now and why? And what are those goals that connect you from where you are right now to that vision? And you're going to ask yourself this really important question. Is it congruent? Is my vision for the future and my current standard congruent? If they're not, we'll have some work to do, but let's not jump ahead. Let's start right here with the questions in your exercises. Make sure you really answer these in a very detailed way after you've done your quiz and slowly but surely as you go through all of these exercises in each lesson, we will unlock the truth about the congruency of your vision to your current standard and if you need to raise it. And if you don't, that's great. But you'll also know now how to raise other people's standards too. And that's an incredibly valuable skill set to possess. Whether you're a business owner, entrepreneur, sales manager, or just a human being moving through the world that wants to be a positive impact on those around you, whether it's your own children, friends, family and so forth. All right. So let's go to our quiz right now and please, please, please take the time to really engage with these exercises and when you're done, I'll see you in the next lesson.
